_advisory_note: F-32 Tier 2 advisory: WA driver-license specimen audit — JSON-only fact-extraction; no specimen images are redistributed. WA legacy PIC filler is moot under the AAMVA M1 envelope substitution (WA ships as envelope; per-state PIC format not reproduced). License-posture distribution: 11 state-work + 26 permissive-OSS-MIT + 3 state-statute-anchored + 11 aamva-envelope = 51; zero needs-legal-review.

rows.license_notes: AAMVA M1 envelope substitution per V1 shipping disposition; canonical envelope per the V1 canonical register; state-format claim retracted; no per-state format reproduced. W-R-3 had `^[0-9]{1,7}$` (visual-only attestation per W-R-5 §3.1); pattern is superseded by envelope.
MIT baseline (gas-buddy/usdl-regex; adambullmer/USDLRegex) attests; ALEA site shows specimen but no prose format. Older aggregators say `{1,7}`; MIT baseline widened to `{1,8}`.

rows.dln_overlap_note: Legacy 9-digit DL is visually indistinguishable from SSN; two-way DLN/SSN ambiguity (was three-way in an earlier revision; D-36 V1-WITHDRAWN). Detector mitigation routes via D-12 / D-16 A21 anchors — no D-36 dependency.
rows.license_notes: AR INA TVR help page (ark.org state portal) prose-defines DL as 9-digit integer; 8-digit legacy normalized to 9 via zero-padding (`012345678`). Promoted from W-R-3 needs-legal-review per W-R-5 §3.2; tightened from {4,9} to {8,9}. Disconfirms aggregator (Skyhigh/MyShn-lineage) claim that AR DLs always begin with 9.
ADOT MVD blog `Evolution of Arizona's Driver License` shows specimens; MIT baseline attests. Legacy 9-numeric (SSN-era) still in circulation.
DMV handbook shows specimen (1 alpha + 7 numeric); 4 independent MIT libs agree; stable for decades.
CO DMV silent; MIT baseline provides 3 alternations; primary form is 9-numeric. Legacy alpha-prefixed variants still circulate.
CT DMV silent on format; industry-wide convergent agreement on 9 numeric. Some legacy CT DLs historically encoded DOB in first 2 digits; shape unchanged.

AAMVA M1 envelope substitution per V1 shipping disposition; canonical envelope per the V1 canonical register; state-format claim retracted; no per-state format reproduced. W-R-3 had `^[0-9]{7}$` with secondary `^[0-9]{9}$` cohort per W-R-5 §3.12; live-vs-legacy unresolvable from primary sources, now moot under envelope.
DE DMV site carries State-of-Delaware footer copyright; factual format not restricted (Feist). MIT baseline attests.

Fla. Stat. § 322.14(1)(a) prescribes format; FLHSMV release confirms shape unchanged and ≥4 digits now randomized post-2024-07-31. F-34: V1 regex shape unchanged; no checksum validation in V1 scope. Legacy algorithmic (Soundex of last name + DOB + gender) still in circulation.
DDS (Driver Services) shows specimen but does not prose-document format. MIT baseline attests. 7-digit legacy numbers still exist.

rows.dln_overlap_note: Legacy 9-digit DL is visually indistinguishable from SSN; two-way DLN/SSN ambiguity (was three-way in an earlier revision; D-36 V1-WITHDRAWN). Detector mitigation routes via D-12 / D-16 A21 anchors — no D-36 dependency.
rows.license_notes: HI DLs are issued at the county level; no centralized format page. MIT baseline attests. Legacy 9-numeric (SSN-era) still in circulation.
Iowa DOT says DL number is assigned but does not publish structure; MIT baseline attests both variants. 3n+2a+4n is the newer RecID form.

rows.dln_overlap_note: Legacy 9-digit DL is visually indistinguishable from SSN; two-way DLN/SSN ambiguity (was three-way in an earlier revision; D-36 V1-WITHDRAWN). Detector mitigation routes via D-12 / D-16 A21 anchors — no D-36 dependency.
rows.license_notes: ITD DMV silent; MIT baseline provides distinctive AA######A format and legacy SSN alt. Legacy SSN-based 9-digit still in circulation.

AAMVA M1 envelope substitution per V1 shipping disposition; aggregator lineage retired per V1 shipping disposition; canonical envelope per the V1 canonical register; state-format claim retracted; no per-state format reproduced. Underlying real-world IL DL is Soundex-derived (per ISP LEADS manual); F-29 elevated PII-severity flagged at row level.
BMV does not document format; printed with dashes as `####-##-####`. Legacy SSN-based 9-digit still in circulation.
KDOR silent; K-prefix variant is dominant today. Multiple legacy variants coexist.
KYTC silent; alpha prefix loosely tracks surname region. KY DL modernization c.2019–2022; number shape unchanged.

rows.dln_overlap_note: Legacy 9-digit DL is visually indistinguishable from SSN; two-way DLN/SSN ambiguity (was three-way in an earlier revision; D-36 V1-WITHDRAWN). Detector mitigation routes via D-12 / D-16 A21 anchors — no D-36 dependency.
rows.license_notes: OMV silent on format; MIT baseline attests; 9-digit is current standard. Historically allowed SSN; current DLs are OMV-assigned.

historical_variants.coexistence_window_notes: S-prefix combinations exhausted; SA-prefix variant added; both coexist until renewal cycle completes.

rows.license_notes: RMV does not document; MIT baseline includes SA-prefix variant added in gas-buddy v1.4.2. F-30: legacy `[0-9]{9}` retained (FP cost already incurred for MS/CT/NM/NY/LA).

MVA IDVR User Guide states `the driver's license number must start with a letter followed by 12 numbers` — explicit state-published prose. Soundex system long-standing; encodes last name + initials + DOB.
BMV silent; MIT baseline attests both forms. Legacy 7-digit + alpha overflow exists.
SOS does not prose-document; Gallian and Highprogrammer document algorithm. MI redesigned cards 2024; number format unchanged.

DPS shows specimen; MIT baseline attests. Transitioned 2004-12-13 from Gallian-style encoded to non-encoded/random; shape unchanged. Legacy algorithmic DLs (pre-2004) still in circulation; F-29 elevated PII-severity for the legacy cohort.

DOR Form 5443 specimen shows current DL as `T123456789`; DOR prose narrates Dec 2012 central-issuance migration + Apr 2017 director-signature removal + Jun 2020 redesign. RSMo § 302.181 delegates format to DOR Director (no statutory format spec). F-26 closed: collapsed from W-R-3 6 alternations to single live `^[A-Z][0-9]{9}$` per W-R-5 §3.3. Pre-2005 SSN-as-DLN cohort expired; pre-Dec-2012 alpha+5-9 alternations are legacy.

rows.dln_overlap_note: 9-digit DL is visually indistinguishable from SSN; two-way DLN/SSN ambiguity (was three-way in an earlier revision; D-36 V1-WITHDRAWN). MS highest-risk per spec §1.15. Detector mitigation routes via D-12 / D-16 A21 anchors — no D-36 dependency.
rows.license_notes: DPS silent; MIT baseline attests 9-numeric. Often printed SSN-style `###-##-####`. Historical SSN-as-DL; modern numbers are not SSN but look like one.

AAMVA M1 envelope substitution per V1 shipping disposition; canonical envelope per the V1 canonical register; state-format claim retracted; no per-state format reproduced. W-R-3 had 4 alternations (^[0-9]{13}$ | ^[A-Z][0-9]{8}$ | ^[0-9]{9}$ | ^[0-9]{14}$); MCA 61-5-111(2) requires only `distinguishing number` (no format spec); the `41-prefix birthdate-embedded 13-digit` narrative traces to non-authoritative aggregators per W-R-5 §3.4 — pattern superseded by envelope.

Anchored to N.C. G.S. § 20-7(n)(7) (format provision; UVC paraphrase as `identifying number`). Re-verified 2026-04-26 against ncleg.gov direct fetch. Range tightened from W-R-3 {1,12} to {8,12} per W-R-5 §3.5 (no NC primary backs <8 digits; aggregator-only lower bound dropped). NC § 20-7(b1)–(b2) mandate SSN/ITIN submission and bar SSN-as-DLN public disclosure but do not specify DL number length, alphabet, or check-digit; the format provision (n)(7) anchors the identifier's existence without format spec.
NDDOT shows specimens; 3-alpha prefix = first 3 letters of last name (padded with X). Mild re-identification risk.
DMV specimen brochure shows sample `N99999999`; federal E-Verify fact sheet corroborates. MIT baseline (gas-buddy v1.0.3) widened alpha form. Legacy 1–7 numeric persists from earlier issuance.

historical_variants.coexistence_window_notes: 10-char name/DOB-derived → 11-char 3A+8N; both valid until renewal cycle completes.

rows.license_notes: DMV press release explicitly states: `three letters and eight randomly assigned digits for a total of 11 characters` (new) vs. `10 digit numbers...based on a person's name and birth date` (legacy). F-29 elevated PII-severity for legacy 10-char name/DOB-derived cohort still in circulation.

AAMVA M1 envelope substitution per V1 shipping disposition; aggregator lineage retired per V1 shipping disposition; canonical envelope per the V1 canonical register; state-format claim retracted; no per-state format reproduced. W-R-3 had `^[A-Z][0-9]{14}$` (15 chars; first char = surname initial; digits encode name/DOB/sex) — outside envelope's 8-13 range, so real NJ DLs of the 15-char form will not match the envelope (accepted V1 detection gap per V1 shipping disposition false-positive-vs-false-negative tradeoff).
MVD online renewal service explicitly states `This 9-digit number can be found at the top of your license.`

AAMVA M1 envelope substitution per V1 shipping disposition; aggregator lineage retired per V1 shipping disposition; canonical envelope per the V1 canonical register; state-format claim retracted; no per-state format reproduced. W-R-3 had 4 alternations (10-digit dominant; 9-digit legacy; 12-digit; X-prefix non-REAL-ID); pattern superseded by envelope.

DMV explicitly: `9 digit DMV ID Number on the front of the document under the phrase DRIVER LICENSE.` The 8-or-10-char `document number` is a separate security field (DD), not the DL number. F-27 closed: OSS-baseline exotic alternations (`^[A-Z][0-9]{18}$`, `^[0-9]{16}$`, `^[A-Z]{8}$`) not present on dmv.ny.gov as primary DL identifiers — treated as legacy/commercial variants outside V1 scope.
BMV shows specimens; 2A+6N is common current form. 2018 central-issuance redesign; format unchanged.

DPS announced new 10-char alpha+9N via news releases c.2014; state portal silent; MIT baseline attests. Transition c.2014: 9N → 1A+9N; legacy 9-numeric still valid. Alpha prefix serves as fraud check.
ODOT DMV shows sample cards; no explicit digit count. MIT baseline (gas-buddy v1.0.2) added `A/B`+6N historical variant. Low lower-bound reflects historical low-serial cards still valid.
PennDOT explicitly: `Your eight (8) digit Driver's License Number (DLN).` The `DD number` on PA cards is a document discriminator, not the DLN.

historical_variants.coexistence_window_notes: RIMS launch July 5 2017; both 7-digit and 8-digit valid until renewal cycle completes.

rows.license_notes: RI DBR Industry Alert 2017-2 explicitly documents 7→8 transition July 5 2017 (RIMS launch). Some sources also report rare 1A+6N variant (unconfirmed; not shipped).

AAMVA M1 envelope substitution per V1 shipping disposition; canonical envelope per the V1 canonical register; state-format claim retracted; no per-state format reproduced. W-R-3 had `^[0-9]{5,11}$`; W-R-5 §3.6 tightened to `^[0-9]{6,11}$` but tightening is superseded by envelope. SC Code § 56-1-90 governs application identification but does not prescribe DL format.

AAMVA M1 envelope substitution per V1 shipping disposition; canonical envelope per the V1 canonical register; state-format claim retracted; no per-state format reproduced. W-R-3 had `^[0-9]{6,10}$|^[0-9]{12}$`; W-R-5 §3.7 recommended striking the 12-digit aggregator-only branch (single commercial-aggregator origin); both per-state forms superseded by envelope. SDCL § 32-12-17.10 requires only `distinguishing number` (no format spec).

Anchored to Tenn. Code Ann. § 55-50-331(b)(1) (`distinguishing number`). Re-verified 2026-04-26 against W-R-5 §3.8 evidence_paragraph verbatim quotation. Range retained at W-R-3 {7,9} per W-R-5 §3.8 (no primary basis to tighten or broaden). TN DSHS distinguishes the 16-char DD (document discriminator) from the DL number; statute does not prescribe length, alphabet, or check-digit.
TxDPS DL status form explicitly: `Drivers License or ID Card # (8 digits number).` Card design changed 2025-08-18 (polycarbonate); number format unchanged.

Anchored to Utah Code Ann. § 53-3-207(3)(a)(i) (`distinguishing number`). Re-verified 2026-04-26 against le.utah.gov direct fetch. Range retained at W-R-3 {4,10} per W-R-5 §3.9 (W-R-5 rules forbid tightening without primary basis; Gallian 1991/2004 Luhn-style claim is academic exposition not Utah primary). Title 53 ch. 3 Parts 2/4/8 do not specify length or character set.
VA DMV AAMVA PDF417 spec doc shows DAQ field example `T16700588` (1A+8N); MIT baseline attests broader alternation. Often hyphenated for display `L##-##-####`. `Customer Number` in VA terminology.

VT DMV MVR help page explicitly: `Vermont PID is either an 8 digit number or a seven digit number followed by the character A.` Gold-standard primary-source state attestation. `A` variant preserved from legacy.

AAMVA M1 envelope substitution per V1 shipping disposition; aggregator lineage retired per V1 shipping disposition; canonical envelope per the V1 canonical register; state-format claim retracted; no per-state format reproduced. W-R-3 had `^WDL[A-Z0-9]{9}$|^(?=.{12}$)[A-Z]{1,7}[A-Z0-9\*]{4,11}$` (post-2018-09-04 DRIVES system + legacy name-PIC with `*` filler). F-31 moot under envelope (legacy PIC `*` does not appear in shipped pattern). F-29 elevated PII-severity retained for legacy PIC cohort still in circulation through ~2024–2026 renewal. F-32 [Tier 2 advisory] WA specimen-image audit per `_advisory_note` at file root.

Federal E-Verify RIDE fact sheet explicitly: `fourteen characters — one letter...` WisDOT itself does not publish; federal fact sheet is authoritative for cross-state. Long-standing Gallian-style Soundex (first letter = soundex of surname); stable for decades. Displayed as `L###-####-####-##`. Attestation is federal §105 PD; license_posture remains permissive-OSS-MIT per the 4-value enum (no `pd-federal-105` slot — federal-section-105 attestation is captured in the attestation field).

AAMVA M1 envelope substitution per V1 shipping disposition; canonical envelope per the V1 canonical register; state-format claim retracted; no per-state format reproduced. W-R-3 had `^[0-9]{7}$|^[A-Z]{1,2}[0-9]{5,6}$`; W-R-5 §3.10 recommended striking the alpha-prefix branch (aggregator-only, NTSI/Wikipedia-upstream); both per-state forms superseded by envelope. WV Code § 17B-2-8 requires only `coded number` (no format spec).

AAMVA M1 envelope substitution per V1 shipping disposition; canonical envelope per the V1 canonical register; state-format claim retracted; no per-state format reproduced. W-R-3 had `^[0-9]{9,10}$`; W-R-5 §3.11 tightened to exactly 9 (federal RIDE fact sheet snippet `nine numbers`); F-33 closed by W-R-5 with WY archived RIDE fact sheet preserved via Wayback (2025-02-01 snapshot). Both per-state forms superseded by envelope. Wyo. Stat. Title 31 Ch. 7 does not prescribe format.
